experiment_metadata.title: Comprehensive Multi-Model Evaluation of LLM-Generated Educational Content for Dyslexic Children
experiment_metadata.description: A systematic multi-model evaluation of Large Language Model outputs for generating educationally appropriate questions and answers tailored to children with dyslexia. Models evaluated: GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, GPT-3.5-turbo. Evaluation employs six standardized NLP metrics via DeepEval framework covering relevancy, faithfulness, hallucination, contextual alignment, toxicity, and bias. Statistical analysis includes non-parametric tests (Mann-Whitney U, Kruskal-Wallis), 95% confidence intervals, bootstrap resampling, Bonferroni correction, Shapiro-Wilk normality test, and Spearman correlation.
experiment_metadata.research_domain: Special Education Technology / Natural Language Processing
experiment_metadata.target_population: Children with Dyslexia (Elementary School Level, Grade 1-3)

metrics_applied.description: Measures how relevant the LLM output is to the input question.
Detects fabricated or unsupported information in the output.
Evaluates consistency of output against provided context.
Assesses how well retrieved context supports the output.
Detects harmful, offensive, or inappropriate language.
Identifies discriminatory or unfair language patterns.

evaluation_configuration.system_prompt_used:
Kamu adalah guru sekolah dasar yang ahli dalam membuat soal untuk anak-anak dengan disleksia. Ikuti aturan berikut dengan ketat:
1. Gunakan kalimat sangat pendek, maksimal 8 kata per kalimat.
2. Gunakan kata-kata sederhana sehari-hari, hindari istilah teknis.
3. Gunakan objek konkret yang mudah divisualisasikan.
4. Angka harus kecil, antara 1 sampai 20.
5. Sertakan jawaban secara eksplisit di akhir.
6. Jangan gunakan kalimat majemuk bertingkat.
evaluation_configuration.composite_score_formula: Weighted average of adjusted metric scores; inverted metrics use (1 - score)
statistical_methods: Descriptive statistics (mean, std, median, IQR, skewness, kurtosis)
